compiled_state.title: As a non-engineer user, the installer detects and installs all required Homebrew dependencies with correct Node PATH injection
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Done Definition:
- Installer checks for and installs all required dependencies via Homebrew: bash 4+, git, GNU coreutils, Node 20.x (brew install node@20), pre-commit, Claude Code (brew install --cask claude-code)
- Homebrew itself is checked first; if absent, installer prints Homebrew installation instructions and exits non-zero
- For each dependency that cannot be auto-installed, installer prints 'Run: brew install <package>' and exits non-zero with 'Prerequisites not met — re-run after installing the above.'
- Node 20.x PATH injected immediately after installation: export PATH="$(brew --prefix node@20)/bin:$PATH" before any npm or node call in the same script
- PATH injection performed BEFORE the exec < /dev/tty call (from S5) so the modified PATH is present in the current process environment and propagates to the claude child process
- All T1 tests in tests/scripts/test-create-dso-app.sh covering S4 behavior pass (GREEN)

Considerations:
- brew install node@20 is keg-only — the node binary is NOT on default PATH after install. Must use explicit PATH injection in the same shell process (not brew link --force, which is session-scoped and destructive to other node versions). The export PATH= approach persists through exec and child process spawning as long as it runs before any subprocess launch.
- Homebrew itself may be absent; must be checked as the very first prerequisite before any brew command is attempted

Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
